members.name: CutoffHz
members.summary: Frequency threshold for the filter, in Hz. Value can range from 1 to 20500.

members.name: Db
members.summary: Steepness of the cutoff curve in dB per octave (twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz]), also known as the "order" of the filter. Higher orders have a more aggressive cutoff.

members.name: Gain
members.summary: Gain of the frequencies affected by the filter. This property is only available for [AudioEffectLowShelfFilter] and [AudioEffectHighShelfFilter]. Value can range from 0 to 4.

members.name: Resonance
members.summary: Gain at or directly next to the [member cutoff_hz] frequency threshold. Value can range from 0 to 1. Its exact behavior depends on the selected filter type: - For shelf filters, it accentuates or masks the order by increasing frequencies right next to the [member cutoff_hz] frequency and decreasing frequencies on the opposite side. - For the band-pass and notch filters, it widens or narrows the filter at the [member cutoff_hz] frequency threshold. - For low/high-pass filters, it increases or decreases frequencies at the [member cutoff_hz] frequency threshold.

values.name: FILTER_12DB
values.summary: Cutting off at 12 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

values.name: FILTER_18DB
values.summary: Cutting off at 18 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

values.name: FILTER_24DB
values.summary: Cutting off at 24 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

values.name: FILTER_6DB
values.summary: Cutting off at 6 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

constants.name: FILTER_6DB
constants.summary: Cutting off at 6 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

constants.summary: Cutting off at 12 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].

constants.summary: Cutting off at 18 dB per octave. One octave is twice the frequency above [member cutoff_hz], or half the frequency below [member cutoff_hz].
